description: Configuration file for the resist-lint custom linter.

$schema.description: Path to the JSON Schema for IDE autocomplete.

exclude.description: Glob patterns to exclude from linting (e.g. "*.test.ts", "*.d.ts").

include.description: Paths to include in linting (relative to workspace root).

overrides.description: File-specific rule overrides. Last matching override wins.

files.description: Glob patterns matching files to apply these overrides to.

rules.description: Rule-level severity overrides for matched files.

ruleOptions.description:
Per-rule configuration options. Keys are rule IDs, values are option objects.

Common options:
- categories: string[] — override rule categories for filtering
- stages: string[] — override pipeline stages (lint, check, pre-commit, build, ci, test)

rules.description:
Rule ID → severity mapping. Unlisted rules default to "error".

Available rules:
- `comments/no-lint-disable`: Forbids lint-suppression comments (eslint-disable, oxlint-ignore, @ts-ignore, etc.)
- `comments/require-blank-line-groups`: Require blank lines between declaration and control flow statement groups
- `comments/require-section-marker-style`: Section markers must use the canonical // === style
- `comments/require-section-order`: File sections marked with // === headers must follow canonical order
- `complexity/array-size-warning`: Warn on unbounded array growth in while(true) loops
- `complexity/no-array-method-in-loop`: Avoid array methods (.find, .filter, .includes, .some, .every) inside loops
- `complexity/no-await-in-loop`: Avoid await inside loops — use Promise.all() for parallel execution
- `complexity/no-concat-in-loop`: Avoid string concatenation (+=) or .concat() inside loops
- `complexity/no-dom-query-in-loop`: Cache DOM queries outside loops
- `complexity/no-filter-map-chain`: Avoid .filter().map() chains — use a single-pass reduce or for...of
- `complexity/no-index-of-in-loop`: Avoid .indexOf() inside loops — use Map or Set instead
- `complexity/no-json-parse-in-loop`: Avoid JSON.parse()/JSON.stringify() inside loops
- `complexity/no-nested-array-iteration`: Avoid nested loops over arrays — O(n²) complexity
- `complexity/no-regex-in-loop`: Compile regex outside loops — avoid new RegExp() in loop body
- `complexity/no-repeated-traversal`: Combine multiple array passes (.filter + .map on same array) into a single pass
- `complexity/no-sort-in-loop`: Avoid .sort() inside loops — O(n² log n) complexity
- `complexity/no-spread-in-reduce`: Avoid object/array spread inside .reduce() — creates O(n²) copies
- `complexity/prefer-map-for-lookup`: Use Map for repeated key lookups instead of multiple .find() calls
- `complexity/prefer-set-for-existence`: Use Set for existence checks instead of .includes() in loops
- `complexity/recursive-depth`: Recursive functions should include a depth/limit parameter
- `directives/max-suppressions-per-file`: Warns when a file has more than 3 @ts-expect-error directives
- `directives/no-biome-ignore`: Forbids biome-ignore directives — fix the code or adjust biome.json
- `directives/no-eslint-disable`: Bans ESLint directives — this codebase does not use ESLint
- `directives/no-generic-any-assertion`: Disallow 'as any' type assertions
- `directives/no-oxlint-ignore`: Forbids oxlint directives — fix the code or adjust oxlint.json
- `directives/no-prettier-ignore`: Bans Prettier directives — formatting is handled by Biome
- `directives/no-suppression-in-new-code`: Warns on @ts-expect-error — new code should be properly typed
- `directives/no-ts-expect-error-on-any`: Warns when @ts-expect-error suppresses errors on code using the any type
- `directives/no-ts-ignore`: Bans @ts-ignore directives — use @ts-expect-error with an explanation instead
- `directives/no-ts-nocheck`: Bans @ts-nocheck directives — file must have type-checking enabled
- `directives/no-type-assertion-chain`: Disallow double type assertion chains (as unknown as / as any as)
- `directives/require-ts-expect-error-reason`: Requires @ts-expect-error directives to include an explanation of at least 10 characters
- `imports/no-barrel-files`: Forbids barrel files (index.ts with re-exports)
- `imports/no-js-extension`: Import paths must not use .js extension — use .ts or omit
- `imports/no-raw-json`: Use safeStringify/parseJsonWithComments instead of JSON.stringify/parse
- `imports/no-raw-node-imports`: Use @/utils/core shared utilities instead of raw node:* imports
- `imports/no-reexport`: Forbids re-exports — always import from canonical source
- `imports/no-relative-imports`: Import paths must not use relative paths (./ or ../)
- `imports/require-import-groups`: Require blank lines between import groups (node, external, workspace)
- `jsdoc/param-type-match`: @param {Type} must match the actual TypeScript type annotation
- `jsdoc/require-example`: Exported functions must have an @example block with ```typescript``` fence
- `jsdoc/require-jsdoc`: Exported functions and types must have a JSDoc comment
- `jsdoc/require-module`: Every TypeScript file must have a top-level /** @module */ JSDoc comment
- `jsdoc/require-param`: Every function parameter must have a matching @param in JSDoc
- `jsdoc/require-returns`: Exported functions with non-void return types must have @returns {Type}
- `jsdoc/require-schema-link`: Types derived from Valibot schemas must include {@link SchemaName} in JSDoc
- `jsdoc/validate-example`: TypeScript code in @example blocks must be syntactically valid
- `naming/camel-case-vars`: Variables and functions must use camelCase
- `naming/constant-screaming-case`: Top-level const with literal init must use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- `naming/pascal-case-types`: Types, interfaces, and enums must use PascalCase
- `naming/svelte-file-pascal-case`: Svelte files must have PascalCase filenames
- `naming/ts-file-kebab-case`: TypeScript files must have kebab-case filenames
- `primitives/division-by-zero`: Potential division by zero - add check for zero divisor
- `primitives/no-array-hole`: Array holes behave differently from undefined - use explicit undefined or remove element
- `primitives/no-array-index-string`: String index on array creates object property, not array element
- `primitives/no-array-length-mutation`: Direct .length mutation can lose data or create holes - use slice/splice
- `primitives/no-bigint-number-mix`: Disallow mixing BigInt and number in arithmetic operations
- `primitives/no-compare-different-types`: Comparison between different types uses implicit coercion - convert explicitly
- `primitives/no-date-arithmetic`: Date arithmetic returns milliseconds - use date-fns or explicit getTime()
- `primitives/no-date-mutation`: Date mutation methods are error-prone - create new Date or use date-fns
- `primitives/no-float-equality`: Disallow direct equality comparison of floating-point numbers
- `primitives/no-in-operator-primitive`: 'in' operator throws on primitives - check type first or use optional chaining
- `primitives/no-infinity-arithmetic`: Disallow operations that may produce Infinity
- `primitives/no-json-bigint`: JSON.parse without reviver may lose precision on large integers
- `primitives/no-json-circular`: JSON.stringify may throw on circular structure - use safe-stringify or handle cycles
- `primitives/no-json-nan-infinity`: JSON.stringify converts NaN/Infinity to null - validate or use replacer
- `primitives/no-json-undefined`: JSON.stringify omits undefined values - use null or replacer function
- `primitives/no-lossy-math-operation`: Lossy rounding pattern for decimal precision - use integers (cents) or decimal library
- `primitives/no-math-random-crypto`: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure - use crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.getRandomValues()
- `primitives/no-modulo-negative`: % operator with potentially negative number - JavaScript % is remainder, not modulo
- `primitives/no-new-date-string-parse`: Date string parsing is inconsistent - use explicit constructor or date library
- `primitives/no-object-prototype-access`: Use Object.hasOwn() instead of obj.hasOwnProperty()
- `primitives/no-regex-on-untrusted`: RegExp with user input is ReDoS vulnerability - escape input or use string methods
- `primitives/no-relational-null-undefined`: Relational comparison with null/undefined has counterintuitive results
- `primitives/no-string-index-unicode`: String indexing may split unicode characters - use [...str] or for...of
- `primitives/no-string-length-unicode`: String .length counts UTF-16 code units, not characters - use [...str].length for unicode
- `primitives/no-toFixed-rounding`: Warn against toFixed() due to inconsistent rounding behavior
- `primitives/no-unsafe-integer`: Disallow integer literals exceeding MAX_SAFE_INTEGER
- `primitives/object-is-for-special`: Use Object.is() for -0 or NaN comparison, not ===
- `primitives/prefer-bigint-for-ids`: Warn when ID fields in interfaces use number type
- `primitives/prefer-math-trunc`: Math.floor() on potentially negative value - use Math.trunc() for consistent truncation toward zero
- `primitives/prefer-normalize-comparison`: String comparison without normalize() - visually identical strings may have different encodings
- `primitives/use-number-is-finite`: Enforce Number.isFinite() over global isFinite()
- `primitives/use-number-is-integer`: Enforce Number.isInteger() over manual x % 1 === 0 checks
- `result/check-before-access`: Result .ok must be checked before accessing .data or .error
- `result/no-ignore-result`: Result return values must be captured — do not ignore them
- `result/no-redundant-ok-guard`: Redundant .ok guard — both branches return the same variable
- `result/no-result-fallback`: Do not silently discard Result errors with fallback values
- `result/no-ternary-fallback`: Forbids result.ok ? result.data : fallback — use if (!result.ok) return result
- `result/require-ok-return`: Functions returning Result<T> must use ok()/okUnchecked()/err() in returns
- `result/require-result-type`: Exported functions that might fail should return Result<T>
- `result/validate-function-input`: Exported/handler functions should validate input with safeParse()
- `testing/multi-export-a`: Multi-export fixture rule A (no-op).
- `testing/multi-export-b`: Multi-export fixture rule B (no-op).
- `testing/named-export-a`: Named export fixture rule (no-op).
- `testing/require-colocated-tests`: Files exporting functions must have a colocated .test.ts file
- `typescript/no-bare-as-cast`: Every `as` cast must have an inline comment explaining why
- `typescript/no-bare-data-types`: Data types must use Valibot schemas, not interface/type literals
- `typescript/no-builtin-types`: Use Valibot types (Str, Num, Bool, Void) instead of TypeScript builtins
- `typescript/no-default-params`: Function parameters must not have defaults or optional markers — use Valibot schemas
- `typescript/no-empty-catch`: Catch blocks must use fromUnknownError() + err() pattern
- `typescript/no-generic-function-type`: Function types must have specific parameter and return types, not generic unknown
- `typescript/no-module-side-effects`: Top-level code must not throw or execute side-effect calls on import
- `typescript/no-throw`: Forbids throw statements — use return err() instead
- `typescript/no-union-null`: Use Valibot nullable/optional types instead of | null / | undefined
- `typescript/no-union-params`: Function parameters must not use | union types — use Valibot schemas instead
- `typescript/require-const-comment`: Every top-level const must have a preceding comment
- `typescript/require-function-schema`: Use functionSchema() from @/schemas/function instead of v.custom for function types
- `typescript/require-non-negative-integer`: .length must be typed as NonNegativeInteger, not Num
- `typescript/require-return-type`: Every function must have an explicit return type annotation
- `typescript/require-svelte-ts-extension`: Files using Svelte runes must use .svelte.ts extension
- `typescript/require-type-annotation`: Every const/let declaration and function parameter must have a type annotation
- `valibot/await-async-parse`: Async parse calls (parseAsync, safeParseAsync) must be awaited
- `valibot/colocate-schema-type`: Type aliases using v.InferOutput<typeof XxxSchema> must colocate the schema
- `valibot/consistent-infer`: Use v.InferOutput consistently; flag v.InferInput unless type name contains "Input"
- `valibot/consistent-nullability`: Do not mix v.optional() and v.nullable() in the same schema object
- `valibot/discriminated-unions`: Use v.variant() for discriminated unions instead of v.union()
- `valibot/error-map-all-locales`: Error maps must include all supported locales
- `valibot/error-map-complete`: Error maps should cover all schema fields
- `valibot/explicit-undefined`: Optional fields should provide a default value — prefer v.optional(schema, default)
- `valibot/export-schema-and-type`: Exported schemas must have a corresponding exported type
- `valibot/import-type-only`: When importing types from valibot, use import type
- `valibot/limit-union-size`: Warns when v.union() has more than 10 variants
- `valibot/namespace-import`: Valibot must be imported as namespace: import * as v from 'valibot'
- `valibot/no-any-schema`: Bans v.any() and v.unknown() — use a specific schema type instead
- `valibot/no-class-validator`: Use Valibot instead of class-validator/class-transformer
- `valibot/no-direct-safeparse`: Forbids v.safeParse() — use safeParse from @/utils/result/safe instead
- `valibot/no-duplicate-keys`: Detects duplicate property keys in v.strictObject() / v.object() calls
- `valibot/no-duplicate-schema`: Schema field patterns appearing in 3+ files should be in @/schemas/common
- `valibot/no-empty-object`: Bans empty v.object({}) and v.strictObject({}) calls
- `valibot/no-expensive-regex`: Warns about potentially ReDoS-prone regex in v.regex()
- `valibot/no-fallback-required`: Bans v.fallback() on required schemas — fallback defeats required validation
- `valibot/no-generic-string-schema`: v.pipe(v.string(), v.minLength(1)) is too generic — add semantic constraints
- `valibot/no-ignore-issues`: safeParse results should check .issues when the parse fails
- `valibot/no-inline-error-message`: Bans inline string error messages in valibot validation methods
- `valibot/no-inline-infer`: Do not use v.InferOutput<> inline in function signatures — declare a type alias
- `valibot/no-io-ts`: Use Valibot instead of io-ts
- `valibot/no-joi`: Use Valibot instead of Joi
- `valibot/no-loose-tuples`: Enforces v.strictTuple() over v.tuple()
- `valibot/no-manual-types`: Object literal type aliases should have a corresponding Valibot schema
- `valibot/no-mutate-after-parse`: Bans mutation of parsed data — validated data should remain immutable
- `valibot/no-nested-optional`: Bans nested v.optional(v.nullable(...)) — use v.nullish() instead
- `valibot/no-omit-pick-infer`: Do not use Omit/Pick on valibot inferred types — use v.omit()/v.pick() instead
- `valibot/no-optional-heavy-object`: Warns when >70% of fields in a schema object are optional/nullable
- `valibot/no-orphan-schemas`: Every exported schema must have a corresponding exported type alias
- `valibot/no-orphan-types`: Type aliases for data shapes should have corresponding Valibot schemas
- `valibot/no-parse`: Forbids v.parse() — use safeParse from @/utils/result/safe instead
- `valibot/no-partial-infer`: Do not use Partial<v.InferOutput<...>> — use v.partial() on the schema instead
- `valibot/no-passthrough`: Bans v.passthrough() — unknown keys should not pass through validation
- `valibot/no-recursive-without-lazy`: Recursive schemas must use v.lazy() to avoid runtime errors
- `valibot/no-reexport-infer`: Do not re-export v.InferOutput/v.InferInput — export concrete types instead
- `valibot/no-schema-in-component`: Bans schema definitions in .svelte files — move to dedicated schema files
- `valibot/no-schema-in-loop`: Bans schema creation inside loops — define schemas once and reuse them
- `valibot/no-transform-side-effects`: v.transform() callbacks must be pure — no console.log, fetch, etc.
- `valibot/no-type-cast-after-parse`: Bans `as` casts on parse/safeParse results — schema already guarantees the type
- `valibot/no-unsafe-coerce`: Bans v.coerce() — coercion silently transforms input and can mask data issues
- `valibot/no-yup`: Use Valibot instead of Yup
- `valibot/no-zod`: Use Valibot instead of Zod
- `valibot/one-schema-per-file`: Files with too many schema definitions should be split
- `valibot/prefer-branded-types`: Use v.brand() for ID/nominal types to prevent accidental mixing
- `valibot/prefer-methods`: Use built-in Valibot methods instead of custom v.transform() calls
- `valibot/prefer-picklist`: Use v.picklist() instead of v.union([v.literal(...), ...]) for literal unions
- `valibot/prefer-pipe`: Use v.pipe() for chained validations instead of deprecated nested patterns
- `valibot/prefer-shared-schema`: Use shared schemas (PathSchema, UrlStringSchema, etc.) for matching field names
- `valibot/prefer-template-literal`: Prefer templateLiteral() over v.pipe(v.string(), v.regex()) for structured string patterns
- `valibot/readonly-parse-result`: Parse results should be declared with const, not let
- `valibot/require-description`: Schemas should have v.description() in their pipe
- `valibot/require-error-map`: Schema files should have error map companions
- `valibot/require-error-mapping`: Use mapIssues() after safeParse failures for user-friendly errors
- `valibot/require-field-docs`: Every property in v.strictObject() must have a JSDoc comment
- `valibot/require-generic-schema`: Schemas used by generic types should use generic() from @/schemas/generic
- `valibot/require-min-length`: Bare v.string() in strictObject fields should have v.minLength(1)
- `valibot/require-schema-suffix`: Valibot schema const names must end in "Schema"
- `valibot/require-strict-object`: Forbids v.object() — use v.strictObject() instead
- `valibot/revalidate-on-change`: Data modified after parsing should be re-validated
- `valibot/schema-file-location`: Schema definitions should live in designated directories or files
- `valibot/schema-type-pair`: Every schema must have a corresponding type derived via v.InferOutput
- `valibot/type-alias-from-schema`: Type aliases for data shapes must be derived from schemas using v.InferOutput
- `valibot/validate-boundaries`: Exported functions should validate inputs at module boundaries
- `valibot/validate-function-output`: Exported functions returning data should validate return values
- `package/no-git-deps`: Disallow git+https:// dependencies
- `package/no-hoisted-dep`: Hoisted dependencies must not appear in sub-package dependencies
- `package/no-peer-deps`: Private workspace packages must not have peerDependencies
- `package/no-root-only-scripts`: qa:format, qa:format:check, qa:lint belong only in workspace root
- `package/no-ts-node`: Disallow ts-node usage in dependencies and scripts
- `package/no-tsc-dependency`: Packages using tsgo should not depend on typescript
- `package/no-vitest-config`: Sub-packages must not have vitest.config.ts — use root config with --project
- `package/no-workspace-dep`: Workspace-root-level deps must not appear in sub-package devDependencies
- `package/no-workspace-self-ref`: Sub-packages must not have workspace:* dependencies — use tsconfig paths
- `package/require-project-test`: Test scripts must use pnpm -w exec vitest run --project <name>
- `package/require-readme`: Every sub-package must have a validated README.md
- `package/require-scope`: Non-root packages must have a scoped name
- `package/require-shared-config`: Config files must use shared config tooling
- `package/require-standard-scripts`: Sub-packages must have all standard scripts
- `package/require-tsgo`: qa:type-check must use tsgo, not tsc
- `package/valid-project-ref`: Scripts with --project must reference a valid vitest project name
- `package/valid-tsconfig`: Sub-package tsconfig.json must extend root and include src

title: resist-lint configuration
